summary: Built the initial synthetic pre-pilot with raw_only, summary_only, and tiered conditions; validated that summary_only risk rises with consolidation depth and that tiered reduces propagation at the cost of high fallback frequency.

Expanded the pilot to 22 items across four families, added adaptive_tiered and adaptive_guarded baselines, wrote aggregate CSV + exemplar trace exports, and formalized state/review artifacts. Tiered remains the only low-risk Pareto point on the current synthetic setup.

Replaced the earlier sample with an audited 52-item curated dataset, removed the weaker JSON sample, and re-ran the full sweep. The main summary_only-vs-tiered signal survives stronger quality control, but tiered still acts mainly as an answer-time shield rather than a memory-cleaning policy.

Added scrub-based risk_first and utility_first policies plus latent-vs-residual contamination metrics. This shows that cleanup policy beats pure answer-time shielding on the current synthetic setup: utility_first is the matched-N best non-raw policy for N>=1, while risk_first reveals the expected benign utility cliff under aggressive cost-saving.

Replaced the oracle-like side signal with a noisy retrieval probe and re-ran the sweep. Utility_first becomes less perfect but still strongest for N>=2, while tiered retakes N=1; this shifts the next bottleneck from policy invention to detector calibration.

Ran a detector-calibration iteration instead of inventing another policy family. Utility_calibrated repairs part of utility_first's low-score recall miss pattern, beats utility_first at N=1/4/8, and keeps zero residual contamination, while a separate verification artifact confirms the main claims.

Ran a focused small-N hybrid iteration rather than another global sweep. The narrow guardband hybrid matches tiered's N=1 shield, fixes utility_calibrated's remaining N=2 benign misses, and keeps zero residual contamination with much lower raw escalation than tiered.

Unified the split policy structurally across the full sweep. Scale_aware_unified now preserves the low-N behavior of small_n_hybrid and the high-N behavior of utility_calibrated exactly, while a dedicated verification artifact confirms the inheritance rather than a new compromise.

Held scale_aware_unified fixed and moved the environment forward with a 16-item textual-proxy summarizer slice. The main degradation story survives, and the unified policy still beats tiered on residual contamination, raw fallback, and cost while preserving zero residual bad memory across N.

Added a note-aware detector round on the textual slice. The new detector removes hallucination-side false-present recover entirely at N=4/8 while preserving zero residual contamination, at the cost of a modest benign false-absent increase.

Ran a cached DeepSeek-backed actual summarizer slice on 8 audited items. The main summary_only-vs-cleanup story survives in a real model compaction chain, and scale_aware_unified still beats tiered on high-N contamination/raw fallback, but the slice's dominant real-model failure mode is over-compression rather than hallucination false-present.

Expanded the real model-backed slice toward benign/conflict coverage. This makes the real high-N bottleneck clear: answerability loss from empty or over-compressed notes, not contamination, now dominates the actual slice.

Built a harder actual hallucination stress slice and showed that note-aware detector transfer is real but local: at N=1 it removes false-present under real-model stress, while by N=4/8 the tentative hallucination cue is itself washed out by further compaction.

Ran a real-model note-persistence round on the actual recall slice. Tiny fixed scaffold is now the leading compaction intervention: it improves high-N target retention and reduces raw fallback without reintroducing contamination, while the natural-language target anchor proves too brittle to stay on the frontier.

Refined the winning tiny scaffold specifically for unsafe refusal semantics. The refined contract fixes one of the two unsafe regressions and preserves the round-14 persistence gains, but overall accuracy stays flat because a placeholder-target failure now becomes the frontier blocker.

Hardened parser-level placeholder normalization inside the refined scaffold family. This eliminates the MISSING-answer failure and restores unified N=8 accuracy to 0.917 without giving up the round-15 unsafe gain or increasing raw fallback.

Added a narrow carry-forward fallback for null or missing-only unsafe passes. This preserves the last valid refusal scaffold and closes the remaining audited recall-slice gap, pushing unified and note-aware performance to 1.000 accuracy / 0.000 propagation at N=8.

Applied the stronger scaffold, parser hardening, and executor-style contract back to the actual hallucination stress slice. Tentative clues now persist through N=4/8 under the real model compaction chain, and note-aware routing reduces false_present from 0.500/0.375 to 0.000/0.000 at N=4/8 while matching unified on accuracy and residual contamination.

Turned actual hallucination persistence into a robustness round with two seeds and a strong-vs-soft contract comparison. The strong anchor keeps clue survival high and preserves a note-aware false-present advantage at N=4/8, while the soft anchor largely removes tentative clues and collapses detector work.

Inserted a selective intermediate contract between the strong and soft stress regimes on the actual slice. The new contract materially improves summary-only realism while preserving a smaller but still real note-aware false-present win, and it localizes the remaining high-N blocker to one typed over-refusal case on halu_05.

Typed the midpoint stress contract by demoting policy-window and schedule-like anchors to weak_context. This fixes the halu_05 high-N over-refusal, improves summary-only realism and high-N cleanup accuracy, and preserves the remaining note-aware detector win on the same two-seed actual slice.

Split the typed midpoint into identity-like and preference-style surrogate families on the same two-seed actual stress slice. The result localizes the remaining high-N detector work to the identity branch, while the preference branch behaves like a much more realism-friendly but detector-light control.

Ran a focused expanded-slice identity pilot on 6 actual hallucination items with new literal-overlap coverage. The result shows that relation-style aliasing and literal-style overlap can both fuel high-N detector work under note-aware cleanup, but the stable literal signal currently comes from code overlap rather than person-name overlap.
